title: Akasaka & Minato Sightseeing Guide: Shrines, Skyscrapers, and Serious Dining
excerpt: A guide to Akasaka's landmarks and restaurants, and the private house rental that puts you steps from all of it.
contentHtml:
Akasaka sits at the center of business and government Tokyo, but its dining scene and nearby green spaces make it just as appealing for visitors with no meetings to attend.

Top Sights

Akasaka Sacas — A shopping and entertainment complex built around TBS Television, with restaurants, event spaces, and seasonal illuminations.

Hie Shrine — A striking shrine reached by a tunnel of red torii gates, a quiet contrast to the office towers surrounding it.

Roppongi Hills — Art museums, observation decks, and high-end shopping, a short trip from Akasaka.

Tokyo Midtown Roppongi — More shopping and dining, plus a large public garden.

The National Diet Building — Japan's seat of government, an interesting architectural landmark even from the outside.

Food

Akasaka is known for its high-end traditional restaurants (ryotei), while Roppongi nearby offers everything from ramen to international fine dining, including the food halls beneath Tokyo Midtown.

Where to Stay

For easy access to all of it, Plat House Akasaka offers a full private house near both Akasaka and Akasaka-mitsuke stations, with a kitchen, washing machine, Netflix, and Nintendo Switch. Direct booking beats OTA rates, with free cancellation up to 7 days out.
